questions.question: Which type of risk CANNOT be eliminated through diversification?
questions.explanation: Market (systematic) risk affects the entire market and cannot be diversified away. You can only manage it through asset allocation and time horizon.

questions.question: A trailing stop loss of 10% on a stock that rises from $50 to $80 will trigger a sell at:
questions.explanation: The trailing stop follows the highest price. At $80, a 10% trailing stop = $80 × 0.90 = $72. It would trigger if the stock drops from $80 to $72.

questions.question: Inflation risk is most dangerous for:
questions.explanation: Cash and low-yield bonds are most vulnerable to inflation risk because their returns may not keep up with rising prices, causing a loss of purchasing power.

questions.question: The '1-2% rule' in position sizing means:
options: Risk no more than 1-2% of your total portfolio on one trade
questions.explanation: The 1-2% rule means risking no more than 1-2% of your total portfolio value on any single position, so multiple losses won't devastate your portfolio.
